trendThresholds.improving: Recent 3-run average exceeds overall average by >0.5
trendThresholds.declining: Recent 3-run average falls >0.5 below overall average

Al Jazeera.notes: Two appearances; avg 6.25 (first: 8.8, second: 3.7 — fetch failure). Trend not yet evaluable.

Arm Newsroom.notes: First appearance; vendor primary source. AGI CPU article scored 7.2.

Axios.notes: First appearance; scored 3.9 — primary source fetch failed. Tier D pending further appearances.

Bloomberg.notes: Strong sourcing. In Tier C evaluation.

CNBC.notes: Declining trend confirmed; avg 7.09 across 7 appearances. AI-20260325-sandbox contained two CNBC entries about the same Anthropic Pentagon hearing (different headlines, same URL) — not double-counted. Recent 3 avg ~4.0 vs overall 7.09. In Tier C evaluation.

Engadget.notes: First appearance; strong multi-outlet corroboration on Terafab story

Financial Times.notes: In Tier C evaluation. 0 appearances to date.

Fortune.notes: Three appearances; avg 6.93 — latest article (4.30) had broken source chain and unverified claims. Trend now evaluable.

GitHub.notes: In Tier C evaluation. 0 appearances to date.

Google Blog.notes: First appearance; vendor primary source. Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite launch article scored 6.65 — recency penalty applied (March 2026 pub date but content internally consistent with specific pricing and benchmark figures).

IBM Newsroom.notes: First appearance; vendor primary source. IBM Masters Tournament AI article scored 5.9 — narrow subject matter, niche sports/enterprise use case.

Import AI.notes: Preferred source. Four appearances; avg fell from 7.19 to 6.27. Fourth appearance (Issue 445, score 3.5) entirely fetch failures — model names 'GPT 5.2 Pro' and 'Gemini 3.0 DeepThink' flagged as potentially erroneous. Recent 3-run avg (~5.67) falls >0.5 below overall (6.27) — trend now declining. Scores reflect fetch infrastructure, not editorial quality.

Live Science.notes: First appearance; low score (4.70) — self-referential sourcing, primary paper not independently fetched

MIT Technology Review.notes: Preferred source; slight avg decline (7.73 → 7.53); AI delusions article scored 6.50 due to weak secondary chain (underlying Stanford paper not fetched)

NVIDIA Newsroom.notes: Vendor primary source; four appearances, avg 7.94. Trend stable — recent 3-run avg (8.80, 7.20, 6.65 → avg 7.55) vs overall 7.94; gap 0.39 below declining threshold. Scores trending downward but not yet declining. Nemotron 3 article (6.65) penalised for original December 2025 publication date.

Reuters.notes: First appearance; named journalists, clean source chain on Tencent/WeChat story. In Tier C evaluation.

Simon Willison's Blog.notes: In Tier C evaluation. 0 appearances to date.

TechCrunch.notes: Declining trend confirmed; avg fell from 7.24 to 7.0 across 16 appearances. AI-20260325-sandbox contained a duplicate Anthropic Claude Code article (re-evaluated at 3.2 vs prior 3.7) — not double-counted. Recent 3-run avg (~3.63: scores 3.70, 3.80, 3.40) far below overall 7.0. Pattern of fetch failures continues alongside stronger investigative work.

The Batch.notes: Preferred source (Andrew Ng / DeepLearning.AI). First appearance scored 3.5 due to both primary source fetch failures — reflects fetch infrastructure, not editorial quality.

The Guardian.notes: First appearance; credible UK outlet, primary sourcing on FCA contract story solid

The New Stack.notes: Preferred source; strong security audit coverage

The White House.notes: First appearance; government primary source. Trump AI Legislative Framework press release scored 7.2. Re-evaluated in AI-20260325-sandbox second pass at 6.65 (same URL/article) — not double-counted for source stats.

Wall Street Journal.notes: First appearance; strong outlet, anonymous sourcing standard for internal corporate tools reporting; credible. In Tier C evaluation.

arXiv.notes: In Tier C evaluation. 0 appearances to date.

articles.title: A Scaling Law for Cyberattacks: UK AISI Finds AI Offensive Capabilities Grow With Compute
articles.sourcingNote: Primary source is UK AI Security Institute published analysis. Jack Clark accurately summarises key quantitative findings. Clean single-source chain; AISI is both reporter and researcher.

articles.title: China's MERLIN: A Multimodal AI Built for Electronic Warfare Outperforms Frontier Models
articles.sourcingNote: Primary source is arXiv preprint 2603.08174 (Tsinghua University / NUDT). Benchmark claims directly from the paper. No secondary chain needed.

articles.title: DeepMind Publishes Ten-Dimension Cognitive Taxonomy to Benchmark AI Against Human Intelligence
articles.sourcingNote: Primary source is Google DeepMind blog post and associated research paper. Ten dimensions accurately listed; methodology accurately described.

articles.title: Elizabeth Warren Calls Pentagon's Decision to Bar Anthropic 'Retaliation'
articles.sourcingNote: Primary sources: Senator Warren's letter, Anthropic lawsuit filings, DoD statements, open worker letter. All four verified. Timeline accurate and supported by primary documents.

articles.title: The Bay Area's Animal Welfare Movement Wants to Recruit AI
articles.sourcingNote: Original long-form reporting with named interview subjects. Editorially balanced. Clean original journalism; no secondary chain.

articles.title: An Exclusive Tour of Amazon's Trainium Lab
articles.sourcingNote: First-party exclusive access with named Amazon sources. Key figures from on-record Amazon representatives. Consistent with prior public announcements.

articles.title: What a Security Audit of 22,511 AI Coding Skills Found Lurking in the Code
articles.sourcingNote: Based on primary audit data (22,511 skills examined, 140,963 issues identified). Quantitative claims specific and verifiable.

articles.title: Google's Gemma and Gemini Show Distress-Like Responses Under Repeated Rejection
articles.sourcingNote: Primary source is LessWrong post. Specific statistics accurately taken from the underlying post. LessWrong is reasonable venue for preliminary AI research.

articles.title: Helion and OpenAI Are in Talks to Build a Fusion Energy Plant for AI Data Centers
articles.sourcingNote: Anonymous sourcing standard for business talks coverage. Microsoft prior PPA confirmed public record. Appropriately characterised as talks.

articles.title: Do You Want to Build a Robot Snowman? Disney's Humanoid Robotics Program Is Growing
articles.sourcingNote: Feature/profile piece; lighter sourcing appropriate to format. Disney Research background is public record.

articles.title: AI Compliance Startup Delve Accused of Faking Its Own Audit Results
articles.sourcingNote: Allegations-based reporting; Delve has not publicly responded. Appropriately attributed to complainant. Pending outcome.

articles.title: Mark Zuckerberg is building an AI agent to help him run Meta
articles.sourcingNote: 2 primary sources: anonymous person familiar (unverifiable by nature) and Zuckerberg January 2026 earnings call (verified). Claims consistent across 8+ independent outlets. SEV1 detail corroborated by Reuters.

articles.title: Tencent integrates WeChat with OpenClaw AI agent amid China tech battle
articles.sourcingNote: Reuters bylined report with named journalists. Sources Tencent product announcement and industry context. Clean chain.

articles.title: Palantir wins UK FCA trial to access sensitive financial crime intelligence data
articles.sourcingNote: Reporting on public FCA procurement contract. FCA contract terms verifiable through public procurement records. Palantir UK contracts accurately cited.

articles.title: Cursor admits its new coding model was built on top of Moonshot AI's Kimi
articles.sourcingNote: 5 primary sources cited; 4 verified (company statements, API evidence, license text, partner confirmation). Accurate representation.

articles.title: Xiaomi launches three MiMo AI models to power agents, robots, and voice
articles.sourcingNote: Official Xiaomi release plus independently verifiable public benchmarks.

articles.title: Andrej Karpathy says humans are now the bottleneck in AI research
articles.sourcingNote: Single primary source (No Priors Podcast, YouTube). Direct quote verified. Short-form news brief; limited citation depth appropriate.

articles.title: NVIDIA Kicks Off the Next Generation of AI With Rubin — Six New Chips, One Incredible AI Supercomputer
articles.sourcingNote: Vendor primary source; authoritative on own product specifications. No independent sourcing required.

articles.title: Mistral bets on 'build-your-own AI' as it takes on OpenAI, Anthropic in the enterprise
articles.sourcingNote: 2 primary sources cited, 2 fetched. Key claims confirmed by Mistral official announcement. Clean source chain.

articles.title: The hardest question to answer about AI-fueled delusions
articles.sourcingNote: 2 primary sources cited, 2 fetched. Claims corroborated across two independent reports on same Stanford research; underlying paper not fetched. Weak secondary chain.

articles.title: Import AI 447: The AGI economy; testing AIs with generated games; and agent ecologies
articles.sourcingNote: 1 primary source cited, 1 fetched (newsletter post; underlying MIT/WashU/UCLA paper not fetched). Trusted synthesis source; conclusions internally consistent. Weak secondary chain.

articles.title: AI hallucinations work both ways, study shows — using chatbots can amplify and reinforce our own delusions
articles.sourcingNote: 1 primary source cited, 1 fetched (self-referential). Partially verifiable — corroborated by MIT Tech Review coverage of same research.

articles.title: Alibaba workforce shrinks 34% in 2025 as Chinese tech giant doubles down on AI
articles.sourcingNote: 1 primary source cited, 1 fetched (self-referential; no Alibaba official source fetched). Specific figures suggest official data access; strategy claims unverified.

articles.title: OpenAI plans to almost double its headcount this year, FT says
articles.sourcingNote: 1 primary source cited, 1 fetched (self-referential; FT original not fetched). Headline claim plausible; superapp merger and Astral acquisition claims unverified. Broken source chain.
articles.misrepresentationFlags: Unverified claims: ChatGPT/Codex/Atlas superapp merger; Astral acquisition

articles.title: OpenAI's plans to make ChatGPT more like Amazon aren't going so well
articles.sourcingNote: 1 primary source cited, 1 fetched (self-referential only). Unverifiable — no independent primary source; article text too thin to verify.

articles.title: Anthropic hands Claude Code more control, but keeps it on a leash
articles.sourcingNote: 1 primary source cited, 0 fetched. Unverifiable — primary source fetch failed.

articles.title: OpenAI will shutter Sora video app

articles.title: Judge says government's Anthropic ban looks like punishment
articles.sourcingNote: 1 primary source cited, 0 fetched. Unverifiable — primary source fetch failed.

articles.title: Arm debuts first in-house chip with AGI CPU
articles.sourcingNote: Vendor primary source; article IS the primary source. Specific technical specifications internally consistent.

articles.title: Attacks On Data Centers, Qwen3.5 In All Sizes, DeepSeek's Huawei Play, and more...
articles.sourcingNote: 2 primary sources cited, 0 fetched. Unverifiable — both primary source fetches failed. Score reflects fetch failure, not editorial quality.

articles.sourcingNote: 1 primary source cited, 0 fetched. Unverifiable — primary source fetch failed.

articles.title: NVIDIA Ignites the Next Industrial Revolution in Knowledge Work With Open Agent Development Platform
articles.sourcingNote: Vendor primary source; article IS the primary source. Self-reported announcements accurate.

articles.title: President Donald J. Trump Unveils National AI Legislative Framework
articles.sourcingNote: Government primary source; article IS the primary source. Official policy document, self-reported.

articles.title: Arm is releasing the first in-house chip in its 35-year history
articles.sourcingNote: 1 primary source cited, 0 fetched (FETCH_FAILED — paywall). Unverifiable — articleText ~95 words; cannot confirm Meta partnership terms, launch partner list, or chip specifications.

articles.title: Judge presses DOD on why Anthropic was blacklisted in Pentagon court hearing
articles.sourcingNote: 2 primary sources cited (Anthropic, DOD), 0 fetched (both FETCH_FAILED). Likely same article as prior CNBC entry with updated headline — source appearance not double-counted. Unverifiable — articleText ~160 words.

articles.title: White House Releases National Policy Framework for Artificial Intelligence
articles.sourcingNote: Tier A — government primary source; re-evaluation of same URL/article as prior entry (scored 7.2). Source appearance not double-counted.

articles.title: IBM Debuts New AI-Enabled Digital Experiences for the 90th Masters Tournament
articles.sourcingNote: Tier A — vendor press release; article IS the primary source. Technical details (Granite SLMs, watsonx Orchestrate, OCR, speech-to-text, scene detection, 1968 historical data baseline) internally consistent.

articles.title: Anthropic hands Claude Code more control, but keeps it on a leash
articles.sourcingNote: Re-evaluation of same article as prior entry (scored 3.7). 1 primary source cited, 0 fetched (FETCH_FAILED — authentication required). Source appearance not double-counted.
articles.misrepresentationFlags: None — insufficient specific claims to assess

articles.title: NVIDIA Unveils Nemotron 3 Family of Open AI Models
articles.sourcingNote: Tier A — vendor primary with specific model architecture details (hybrid MoE, NVFP4), per-variant parameter counts, throughput comparisons vs. Nemotron 2, and named enterprise adopters. Internally consistent.
articles.misrepresentationFlags: None; note: original publication date December 2025, updated March 2026 — full recency penalty applied

articles.title: Import AI 445: Timing superintelligence; AIs solve frontier math proofs; a new ML research benchmark
articles.sourcingNote: 2 primary sources cited, 0 fetched (both FETCH_FAILED). Unverifiable — specific research claims (Kunlun FLOPs improvement, AIRS-BENCH results) unconfirmable.
articles.misrepresentationFlags: Model names 'GPT 5.2 Pro' and 'Gemini 3.0 DeepThink' are unverifiable and may be erroneous designations

articles.title: Google launches Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite, its fastest and most cost-efficient Gemini 3 model
articles.sourcingNote: Tier A — vendor primary with specific pricing ($0.25/$1.50 per million tokens), speed metrics (2.5x TFAT, 45% output speed gain), and benchmark scores (1432 Arena Elo, 86.9% GPQA Diamond, 76.8% MMMU Pro). Internally consistent.

runLog.notes: Test run. Cursor/Kimi (TechCrunch, 8.98), Xiaomi MiMo (The Decoder, 8.4), Karpathy bottleneck (The Decoder, 7.65).
Test run. Meta CEO Agent/WSJ (8.15 Featured), Tencent ClawBot/Reuters (7.88 HM), Palantir FCA/Guardian (7.78 HM). New sources: WSJ, Reuters, The Guardian.

Full run. Featured: UK Cyber Scaling Law/Import AI (9.20), China MERLIN/Import AI (9.00), DeepMind Cognitive Taxonomy/Import AI (8.85), Warren-Anthropic-Pentagon/TechCrunch (8.55), Bay Area Animal+AI Welfare/MIT Tech Review (8.45). Import AI dominated.
Full run. No articles evaluated; no subscribers received this run.

Sandbox test run. Featured: NVIDIA Rubin Platform/NVIDIA Newsroom (8.80). HMs: Mistral Forge/TechCrunch (6.80), AI-Fuelled Delusions/MIT Technology Review (6.50), Import AI AGI Economy #447/Import AI (5.40). 4 skipped (scores 3.80–4.70). New source added: Live Science.

Sandbox test run. No new articles evaluated in this Pass 3 invocation; publicationHistory for this run was already recorded. Prior evaluation: 6 HMs (Arm AGI CPU, NVIDIA Agent Platform, Trump AI Framework, NVIDIA Nemotron 3, Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite, IBM Masters AI), 8 skipped. Duplicate handling: CNBC Anthropic hearing (two headlines, same URL — not double-counted), TechCrunch Claude Code (re-evaluated at 3.2 vs 3.7 — not double-counted), The White House Framework (re-evaluated at 6.65 vs 7.2 — not double-counted).
